meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Overcome 'No Project Ideas' as a Founder
content.seo_description: Stop searching for ideas and start shipping. Use our SaaS starter kit, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build profitable products at record speed.
content.answer_block: The 'no idea' paralysis stems from over-analyzing instead of shipping. Solve this by deploying a SaaS starter kit that automates authentication, payments, and database schema. Use Antigravity to scaffold your MVP and Claude Code to iterate. Focus on solving a specific, boring problem for a niche audience to ensure monetization.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank terminal, the cursor blinking like a metronome counting down your wasted potential. You want to build, but the myth of the 'perfect idea' has paralyzed your ambition. Every day you spend searching for a groundbreaking concept is a day your competitors are capturing market share with simple, functional software.

True founders don't wait for inspiration; they engineer it. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you bypass the soul-crushing boilerplate of authentication and billing, moving straight to product-market fit. This isn't about building the next unicorn; it's about building a machine that generates revenue while you sleep.

steps.title: Define Your Micro-Niche
steps.description: Stop looking for 'big' ideas. Use Antigravity to analyze high-frequency, low-complexity problems in professional niches like legal, accounting, or logistics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze market trends for SaaS micro-tools in the accounting sector. Identify 3 high-pain problems with low technical complexity that can be solved with a simple CRUD interface.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a market analysis report for the identified niche.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Generate your foundational codebase. This ensures your stack is production-ready from line one, avoiding the 'hobby project' trap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a SaaS starter kit using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Include pre-configured auth, user dashboard, and subscription billing logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project structure based on the scaffold requirements.'

steps.title: Implement the Core Value Proposition
steps.description: Focus strictly on the one feature that solves the user's problem. Avoid scope creep by keeping the UI minimal and the logic robust.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a schema and API endpoint for the core functionality: processing user-uploaded CSV data into formatted reports.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the data processing service and connect it to the database.'

steps.title: Integrate Monetization Hooks
steps.description: If users aren't paying, you're building a hobby. Integrate Stripe immediately to validate your value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Stripe webhooks to handle subscription tiers and restrict dashboard access for non-paying users.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement Stripe Checkout flow and link subscription status to user middleware.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Ship the code. Perfection is the enemy of revenue. Deploy early to get feedback from real users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment configuration for Vercel with environment variables for database and payment secrets.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run deployment scripts and verify production build status.'

pro_tips: Always charge from day one; if users won't pay for the MVP, they won't pay for the final product.
Ignore design systems until you have at least 10 paying customers; use standard UI components to save time.
Focus on 'boring' SaaS—the less exciting the niche, the less competition you will face.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests; it forces you to define your logic clearly before execution.
Monitor your churn rate immediately; it is the only metric that tells you if your product actually solves the problem.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, spend 80% of your time on distribution and cold outreach. Do not add features; find 10 people who have the problem you solved and ask them to pay for a subscription.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect moment. The market rewards those who ship, not those who dream. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
